title: Session logs are the only place to see agent dispatch details — no tool to query them
description: Agent dispatch details (model, provider, token usage, raw API response, actual agent output) are only available in .pi/agent/sessions/<project>/<timestamp>.jsonl files. There is no tool to query or inspect recent dispatch results. The orchestrator must grep session logs manually to find what an agent produced.
actual_behavior: After call-agent returns a status summary, the only way to see the agent's output (e.g., 'I will start by reading...') is to grep the session log for the toolName call-agent entries and manually parse the JSONL.
expected_behavior: A tool like dispatch-history or agent-traces that returns recent agent dispatch results with their outputs, models, and token usage. Or call-agent should include the output in its result.
reproduction: 1. Dispatch an agent. 2. Try to see what it produced. 3. No tool available — must read session logs.
